title: geum × cebennense
intro: Geum × cebennense, also known as the Cevennes avens, is a hybrid plant species within the geum genus, native to the Cévennes Mountains in southern France. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Geum × cebennense include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, Geum × cebennense is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from digestive issues and other health concerns.
